mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of instances following the unemployment threshold breach, invalidating the recession risk channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates resilient manufacturing output despite tighter financial conditions, supporting short-term equity momentum through improved earnings revisions over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns during tightening periods, with a t-statistic below -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of months following inflation acceleration above the threshold, breaking the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending capacity and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and short-term equity price appreciation over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal supply-side contraction and weakening business investment, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >65% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
